symptom: Known issue where if customers try to use ADFS Authentication through an ILB/VPN, they are unable to. Topology: Local Client -> VPN -> ILB -> ADFS server(s)
rootCause: The issue is due to a problem with how ILBs handle the jumbo packets sent by ADFS servers as SSL "Server Hello. Certificates". Once they reach the Azure Gateway, they are not fragmented properly so they do not make it across the VPN tunnel.
solution: Reduce the MTU packet size on the ADFS servers by executing: netsh interface ipv4 show subinterfaces / netsh interface ipv4 set subinterface "Sub-interface name from previous command" mtu=1350 store=persistent

symptom: Windows 10 Azure AD Joined client using AOVPN to connect to On-Prem resources for SSO. Users are prompted for credentials when accessing On-Prem resources like websites with Integrated Authentication or DFS shares (e.g. \contoso.com\share$). After entering credentials manually, authentication succeeds.
rootCause: VPN Client using AOVPN stores a Cached Credential in Credential Manager built from a Short Lived Certificate issued by Azure VPN Gateway. The certificate Subject Name uses the Azure Tenant Name (e.g. UserX@fabrikam.local). When Kerberos tries to perform DC discovery, it looks for domain 'fabrikam.local' (Azure Tenant Name) instead of the actual On-Prem domain (contoso.com), fails with error 0xC000005E - cannot locate domain controller.
solution: Option 1: Disable cached credentials via registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\disabledomaincreds=1 (drawback: disables all cached creds). Option 2: Modify PBK file at C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Network\Connections\Pbkasphone.pbk, set UseRasCredentials=0 (ideal but requires Intune to deploy). Option 3: Create a logon task via Task Scheduler running 'cmdkey /delete /RAS' to delete only RAS credentials at logon.
